sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient reports feeling short of breath and experiencing sudden episodes of shortness of breath for which he is unsure of the reason.
He mentions that he has a history of heavy drinking and has experienced diarrhea after heavy drinking.
He has also noticed that his symptoms occur in various situations, including at home when the cat is not around, outside, and when he is near his cat.
He denies any symptoms of nausea, vomiting, rash, lip or throat swelling, and allergies.
He has a history of using an inhaler prescribed by the urgent care for his symptoms.
He also mentions that he has a refrigerator full of venison, indicating that he is a hunter.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications: I prescribed albuterol inhaler as needed for asthma symptoms.
The patient is already taking this medication.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs were not mentioned in the transcript, so I do not have any information to document.
Physical exam: The patient's physical exam revealed faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields.
There was no rash, angioedema, or audible stridor.
Test Results: The patient had a chest x-ray and pulmonary function test, both of which were normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma.
Problem list: Allergic asthma; unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed Singulair 10mg daily to help decrease the occurrences of asthma symptoms.
I also plan to proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing, to target the triggers of the patient's asthma.
We will schedule the skin testing for next week.
The patient should continue taking his albuterol inhaler as needed.
Follow-up: I will see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
